name: Prometheus's Fire
summary: The Titan Prometheus steals the gods' fire and gives it to humanity. Zeus chains him to the Caucasus mountain; every day an eagle eats his liver, which grows back at night.
depthMeaning: Fire here is not merely fire — it is consciousness, technology, art, all knowledge taken from 'above'. The Promethean moment is the moment of refusing rote acceptance and securing your own illumination. The liver regrowing at night says: creative pain is repeated but not exhausted. Every great act of creation has a Promethean price.
jungian: For Jung, Prometheus is the carrier of light stolen from the unconscious. 'Lighting your own fire' is the first step of individuation. The archetype of the courage that stands against authority.
dreamMeaning: Stealing fire in dreams, reaching for forbidden knowledge, being chained to a mountain, an eagle harassing you — the Promethean call. The unconscious says 'you are lighting your own fire and paying the price'. In a dream the liver is the seat of heart and courage; renewal happens there.
wakingMeaning: When you act by owning rather than hiding a truth or talent in your life, the response from your environment is harsh. This is the Promethean state — they say 'you don't know your place'. The pain is normal, no going back; but the renewal also continues.
lesson: Creating is always a little like rivalling the gods. Be ready to pay the price — but own it.

name: Inanna's Descent
summary: The sky goddess Inanna descends to the underworld ruled by her sister Ereshkigal. At each of the seven gates she leaves a jewel, a garment. Naked, dead, she is judged; then resurrected.
depthMeaning: In the Inanna myth the seven gates are seven false identities — rank, beauty, status, possession. The 'real you' descends naked into the underworld. The feminine version of death and resurrection, written thousands of years before the Christ myth.
jungian: A classic individuation journey: persona layers are peeled, you meet the shadow (Ereshkigal), you rise as Self. Jung himself saw this myth in the dreams of many patients.
dreamMeaning: Descending a staircase in dreams, removing your clothes one by one, passing through gates, feeling naked, descending to an underground kingdom of women — Inanna signs. Each gate a bond. Feeling dead is not frightening — resurrection is near.
wakingMeaning: If life is stripping you — job loss, breakup, illness, ageing — this is the Inanna descent. The moment you think you've lost everything, you're actually leaving an unnecessary mask at each gate. You may feel dead for three days. Patience: resurrection is not automatic — but very clean.
lesson: To descend into a depth you must accept losing what you have. No jewel descends with you; but on return there's a more real jewel.

name: Epic of Gilgamesh
summary: King Gilgamesh is shaken by the death of his friend Enkidu; he sets out to seek immortality. In the end he learns immortality is not in human hands but friendship and works remain.
depthMeaning: Humanity's first great written text: fear of death, meaning of friendship, permanence of work. Gilgamesh cannot become immortal but leaves the walls of Uruk in writing — writing is the first attempt at immortality.
jungian: Enkidu is Gilgamesh's nature-side, his shadow. The wild man becomes civilised and dies; when Gilgamesh loses him he takes a step into real maturity. Integration of persona and shadow.
dreamMeaning: A loved one's death in dreams (especially symbolic), a long journey searching for immortality, waters, ships, the herb / plant of life — Gilgamesh archetype. Your fear of death may rise to the surface.
wakingMeaning: If a loss has forced you to ask existential questions — especially after a close death — you are at the Gilgamesh point. The answer is not 'not dying' — it is loving, writing, releasing.
lesson: We cannot be immortal but we create meaning through loved ones and works. Not fear, but the maturity of release.

name: The Dismemberment of Osiris
summary: The god Osiris is killed by his brother Seth and torn into 14 pieces, scattered across Egypt. His wife Isis gathers the pieces, reassembles them, briefly revives him. Osiris becomes king of the underworld.
depthMeaning: The Osiris myth says every dismemberment can be brought back together. Isis (divine feminine) is the consciousness that gathers the broken. One of the oldest examples of death-resurrection cycles before Christianity.
jungian: Gathering what is fragmented in the unconscious (trauma fragments). Isis's work is exactly 'shadow integration' — recalling lost pieces.
dreamMeaning: Being torn apart in dreams, your body splitting, gathering pieces of an old body, your spouse / loved one doing this to you — Osiris archetype. Trauma work has begun or is about to.
wakingMeaning: If an area of life feels shattered — divorce, accident, betrayal — take the Isis position: gather piece by piece. You won't become whole at once; but slowly you are recalled. The process of becoming underworld king begins.
lesson: Being broken is not the end; the act of gathering is sacred. You can re-join with love what was scattered in pain.

name: Pandora's Box
summary: The gods give the first woman Pandora a sealed box (actually a jar/pithos) and forbid her to open it. She opens it; evils and diseases spread into the world. The moment the box closes, only hope remains inside.
depthMeaning: The myth seems to blame women, but a deeper reading is different: the development of consciousness comes through breaking taboos. Every enlightenment brings 'the burden of its knowledge'. Hope remains with the evils — because hope too is an illusion but lets us survive.
jungian: Touching the forbidden unconscious — the warning before shadow work begins. The more you say 'don't open' the more it is invited.
dreamMeaning: A closed box in dreams, a chest, a jar, a forbidden opening, contents scattering after opening — Pandora archetype. You are preparing to open something in your unconscious. Don't fear — hope is in the box too.
wakingMeaning: Have you learned a truth you didn't want to know? Have you revealed a secret? The cost is paid but there is no going back. Pandora means this gift is in you. Hold to hope.
lesson: Knowledge is not free. But hope remains last — because it is the deepest one.

name: Theseus and the Minotaur
summary: The hero Theseus goes to slay the half-bull Minotaur living in the Cretan labyrinth. Following Ariadne's thread he exits the labyrinth; he kills the monster at its own centre.
depthMeaning: The labyrinth is the map of the unconscious. The Minotaur is the animal, shameful, half-human/half-beast piece we repress in us. To kill it you must descend to the centre; Ariadne's thread is the cues that keep you tied to the outer world during the process (therapy, friend, ritual).
jungian: The Minotaur is a perfect shadow symbol: unaccepted, kept closed, yet causing pain. Theseus is consciousness's conscious descent into the labyrinth.
dreamMeaning: A labyrinth in dreams, endless corridors, an underground structure with a monster inside, getting lost, following a thread — Theseus myth. The unconscious invites you to face a shadow.
wakingMeaning: If you live the same pattern over and over (same type of relationship, same kind of work crisis) you are in the labyrinth. The exit is not 'fleeing up', but descending to the centre. Defeating a truth there.
lesson: You don't escape the labyrinth — you exit by passing through the centre. Friends and therapists who don't break your thread are precious.

name: Orpheus and Eurydice
summary: The musician Orpheus loses his wife Eurydice to a snake bite. He descends to the underworld and softens Hades's heart with music. Eurydice will return — the condition: don't look back. On the last step Orpheus looks back; Eurydice is lost forever.
depthMeaning: This myth is about grief, loss, and 'not letting go'. Orpheus's looking back isn't the power of his love — it's not being ready to let go. Some returns are made by those who know not to look back.
jungian: The retreating spiritual side (anima) vanishes the moment it is observed. The unconscious feels itself being watched but withdraws if chased; patience and surrender are needed.
dreamMeaning: Following a loved one in dreams but losing them when you turn, opening a door with music, trying to leave the underworld — Orpheus. Mourning is in process but so is clinging.
wakingMeaning: Does a loss feel like 'just a little more' will return it? Phone call, message, looking back — Orpheus turning back. Releasing is painful; but not releasing is a second loss.
lesson: Some losses are accepted by not turning back. Music (art, expression) softens the underworld; but the condition of return is release.

name: Narcissus and Echo
summary: The beautiful youth Narcissus falls in love with his reflection in a pool, cannot leave, and dies of longing. The nymph Echo who loved him becomes a being that can only echo voices.
depthMeaning: This myth describes modern narcissism: obsessive self-love, inability to touch another. Echo is loss of boundary: repeating another without having a voice of one's own. Both are two faces of the same wound — unable to form genuine bonds.
jungian: Persona-Self confusion. The ego mistaking its reflection for reality. Echo is the lost animus/animus, the absence of one's own voice.
dreamMeaning: Getting stuck at a mirror / water surface in dreams, your voice echoing, repeating another's words — Narcissus-Echo axis. Imbalance in how you look at yourself or bond with another.
wakingMeaning: In the social-media age the Narcissus myth is lived daily — if you get stuck in your reflection, if you become Echo who lost her voice, both are the same pain. Genuine bond is neither only at yourself nor only at the other; it is creating a space between.
lesson: Do not fall in love with your reflection, do not lose your voice. Love is being able to touch another.

name: Yggdrasil — The World Tree
summary: Yggdrasil is the giant ash tree holding the nine worlds together. Its roots in Niflheim, its branches in the sky. An eagle at the top, a serpent at the bottom, a squirrel carrying news between.
depthMeaning: The World Tree is the axis of the universe, the axis mundi. It appears in many cultures (the Tuba tree, the tree of the Sefirot). The layered ranks are layers of consciousness: roots the unconscious, branches the superconscious.
jungian: One of the strongest symbols of the Self. The individual psyche is like Yggdrasil — roots in the collective unconscious, branches in personal goals.
dreamMeaning: Seeing a great tree in a dream, sitting beneath it, climbing its branches, seeing its roots — Yggdrasil archetype. You are called toward wholeness.
wakingMeaning: Is your life scattered in many directions? Yggdrasil calls you 'remember the centre'. When making a decision ask: which branch does this feed? Which root does it touch?
lesson: Life is multi-layered — underworld and sky cannot be separated. Stay on your central axis.

name: Ragnarök
summary: Ragnarök is the end of the gods and the world. The wolf-Fenrir breaks free of his chain, the Midgard serpent rises from the sea, the world burns and is submerged. But then a new world is born.
depthMeaning: Norse eschatology is not apocalypse but cycle. The new world is born from a seed — two humans survived and will procreate. There is no concept of 'end'; there is transformation.
jungian: Collapse of the old Self, birth of the new Self. Every shedding of skin in life has its Ragnarök state.
dreamMeaning: End of the world in dreams, flood, fire, an earthquake destroying everything, but at the end a re-beginning landscape — Ragnarök. A personal cycle is closing.
wakingMeaning: If an area of life is completely collapsing (old identity, relationship, career) don't fear — it is not only end, but new beginning. There was no 'permanent' anyway.
lesson: Every end is a beginning. What you will lose was already temporary.

name: Phoenix / Anka
summary: The phoenix sets itself on fire every 500 years and is reborn from its ashes. Same bird, but renewed state.
depthMeaning: The phoenix is the purest symbol of the death-birth cycle. The one who dies of its own will, born of its own ash. 'The being remains the same but the state changes' — the persisting essence.
jungian: The purest form of the transformation archetype. Consciously ending one period of yours and entering another.
dreamMeaning: A fire bird in dreams, burning yourself but not being annihilated, emerging from ashes, sprouting wings — Phoenix. You are at the threshold of a great transformation.
wakingMeaning: A side or period is turning to ash; don't fear — your main essence won't burn, only your old state. In the silence after the fire you'll feel a new wing.
lesson: The new is not born until you willingly burn your old. Don't resist; surrender to the ash.

name: Sisyphus's Rock
summary: For deceiving the gods, Sisyphus is condemned eternally to roll a rock up a hill, then to do it again when the rock falls.
depthMeaning: Camus reads Sisyphus as a metaphor for modern man: making meaning inside meaningless labour. The rock never staying at the top is not hopeless — 'one must imagine Sisyphus happy as he descends the hill', he says.
jungian: Repeating patterns (complexes). Same pain, same motif, but if processed more consciously each time it grows.
dreamMeaning: Doing the same task again and again in a dream, something always falling to the same place, an endless effort — Sisyphus. The unconscious tells you you're stuck in a cycle.
wakingMeaning: Are you having the same argument every year? Repeating the same job at a different company? The lesson of your Sisyphus state: don't blame the rock, change your relationship.
lesson: Meaning comes from inside, not outside. Consciousness can change within repetition.

name: Origin — Leadership of the Grey Wolf
summary: When the lineage is destroyed, the Turks survive in a dry cave. A female Grey Wolf guides them, carrying the lineage out of Ergenekon. The Turks melt an iron mountain and emerge into the world again.
depthMeaning: The Origin myth is both origin and rebirth narrative. The Grey Wolf is both totem and inner guide. Melting the iron mountain is overcoming the impossible with resolve.
jungian: The wolf is a totem animal: personal and collective guiding energy. The Ergenekon cave is womb / dark passage / underworld journey.
dreamMeaning: A grey wolf in a dream, exiting a cave, melting iron, bonding with an old lineage — Origin signs. The ancient guidance inside you speaks.
wakingMeaning: If you bond with traditional roots, see your family or culture differently, this archetype is open. Receiving ancient wisdom without leaving modern identity — Origin's today.
lesson: Carry your roots, step into the future. There is a guide-animal inside; listen to it.

name: Erlik Khan and Ülgen — Shamanic Cosmos
summary: In the sky Ülgen represents good, in the underworld Erlik represents evil. The shaman is the bridge between them; through their drum they open the way. They retrieve sick souls from Erlik.
depthMeaning: This myth deifies good and evil while seeing them as inseparable rather than independent. The shaman is wise not alone but because they walk the two poles. Wholeness is not rejection of opposites but their dance.
jungian: Classical opposite-archetype balance. Neither Ülgen alone nor Erlik alone — the shaman's way is to build a bridge between them.
dreamMeaning: Drum sounds in dreams, a bipolar landscape, travel between sky and earth, meeting white and black figures — shamanic cosmos signs. There is a call to mediation inside.
wakingMeaning: If you oscillate between two extremes in life (good-bad, light-darkness, devout-rebellious) instead of choosing you must learn the bridge between them. The shamanic middle way.
lesson: Good and evil are not opposites but complementary. Real adulthood recognises both.

name: Khidr — The Green Man
summary: Khidr is an immortal saint / prophet who greens where he steps, who drank the water of life. He helps the distressed at unexpected moments, is tested.
depthMeaning: Khidr is the inner guide — the sudden inspiration the mind can't explain, the unexpected helper, the stranger who appears in a hard moment. 'He arrived like Khidr' — an idiom in everyday speech — is a psychological reality.
jungian: The Islamic face of the Wise Old archetype. At the same time the Self's intervention in hard moments.
dreamMeaning: Someone in green in a dream, a stranger arriving in help, a mysterious guide, a figure where they touch comes alive — Khidr archetype. The unconscious sends 'help is near'.
wakingMeaning: Did someone / something arrive at your moment of greatest squeeze? Did an unexpected gift, message, solution come? Khidr energy is active. Be grateful, observe, remember — this is also a giving.
lesson: Even in the darkest moment there is help. Learn to make room for the guide that arrives when you are open.

name: Mesnevi — The Ney's Story
summary: Beginning the Mesnevi, Rumi tells the story of the Ney: the reed cut from the reed-bed; its cry is the longing of all souls separated from their root.
depthMeaning: The Ney is the soul itself: cut from the source, moaning to return. Rumi says 'listen to how this ney complains'. That moan is in fact music, art, prayer. Lack and longing are the fuel of creativity.
jungian: The longing of the ego cut from the Self to 'return home'. Individuation is the descent of this.
dreamMeaning: A flute / ney sound in a dream, seeing a reed bed, a feeling of separation, an unreasoned sadness — the Mesnevi archetype. Longing for the main root speaks.
wakingMeaning: Is there an unreasoned longing inside? A feeling of belonging nowhere? Sufis call it 'hicran' — the conscious remembering of being cut from the source. It is not healed, only turned into song.
lesson: The longing itself is already the answer. You are not lost — you are remembering.

name: Köroğlu
summary: Köroğlu, whose father is blinded, climbs to the mountains and becomes the defender of the oppressed people. He strikes the unjust and distributes to the poor. He is famous for his horse Kırat.
depthMeaning: Köroğlu is the folk-hero archetype — Anatolia's cousin to Robin Hood. The representation of the era when justice was secured not by institutions but by personal courage. The voice inside us that says 'the system is unjust, I will fix it'.
jungian: Rebel + Hero combination. Family wound (blind father) coincides with systemic oppression and Köroğlu is born.
dreamMeaning: A mountain fortress in a dream, journey with Kırat, standing against injustice, a duel with a tyrant — Köroğlu archetype. The voice of justice inside wants to rise.
wakingMeaning: Have you witnessed an injustice lately? Are you saying 'someone has to'? Köroğlu says: sometimes that 'someone' is you. But prepare your Kırat (your strength, mind, comrades) before climbing the mountain.
lesson: Comes the moment when justice cannot be left to another. That moment is before your courage.

name: Yunus Emre — Half Apple
summary: Yunus Emre, Anatolia's most beloved folk poet/dervish. 'Whoever does not look upon the seventy-two nations with one eye is a rebel from truth even if a teacher to the people.' Half an apple, winning a heart; great truth in the small.
depthMeaning: Yunus's path is not the path of great theories but of the simple heart. When he says 'you cannot fit into this world' he sees the human as infinite. He counts ignorance not as lack of knowledge but as lack of love.
jungian: The Anatolian face of the saint archetype. Folk wisdom — inner knowledge deepened without reading and writing.
dreamMeaning: Meeting a dervish in a dream, a simple village/tekke, a shared simple meal, a calligraphic poem — Yunus archetype. A call to simplicity is inside.
wakingMeaning: Is your life too complicated? Yunus says: half an apple is enough. A small kindness, an embrace, a true word is more valuable than great theories.
lesson: Truth is not under big words, but in small actions. Heart knowledge stands before book knowledge.

name: Shahmaran
summary: Shahmaran (Queen of Snakes) is a wise and compassionate woman-snake queen. Cemşap who meets her learns healing and knowledge; Shahmaran is killed because the secret was told. Her flesh is poison to the bad, healing to the good.
depthMeaning: In the Shahmaran myth feminine wisdom is in half-human, half-snake form — close to earth, hidden, deep. Killing her is repressing the unconscious; at the same time the patriarchal fear of the knowing woman.
jungian: Anima + Snake = the feminine form of deep wisdom. Killing her is repressing the unconscious; her knowledge continues even by leaks.
dreamMeaning: A half-woman half-snake in dreams, a wise woman in a hidden cave, carrying/revealing a secret — Shahmaran myth. A call to the feminine wisdom inside.
wakingMeaning: Is there a knowledgeable but hidden female figure in your life? (Could be you.) She hides her knowledge but everyone senses it. Shahmaran means carrying wisdom is both honourable and risky.
lesson: Wisdom does not have to be hidden. But when you open, do not assume the world is ready.

name: Krishna's Childhood
summary: Krishna is born just as King Kamsa is about to kill him. Smuggled to the villages, he tends cows in his childhood, bewitches the gopis (milkmaids) by playing the flute. God in the form of a small child.
depthMeaning: The Krishna myth says the divine is hidden in everyday life — the supreme is born as a village child. The flute-playing god is the inner voice calling with love. The desire of the gopis is the metaphor of mystic love.
jungian: The complementary form of the Holy Child archetype. The divine is not crushing but close, warm, playful.
dreamMeaning: Flute sounds in dreams, a blue child, a figure among cows, running maidens — Krishna myth. The small and warm face of the divine speaks.
wakingMeaning: Has life become very serious? Krishna says: the divine is also in play. A child's laugh, music, a dance — these are as sacred as spiritual practice.
lesson: God can be simple, can be playful. Learn to seek the divine in music as well as on mountains.

name: Buddha's Enlightenment
summary: Prince Siddhartha leaves the palace, performs austerities, then finds the middle way. He sits under the Bodhi tree, does not surrender to the attacks of Mara (the god of illusion), and at dawn he awakens.
depthMeaning: In the Buddha myth enlightenment is not flying — it is staying right there. Mara's attacks (fears, desires, doubts) come not from outside but from the mind. Buddha who says 'I did not move' is steady; therefore he opens.
jungian: Full realisation of the Self. Mara's armies are the final attack of inner complexes. Buddha solves them not by fighting but only by recognising.
dreamMeaning: Sitting under a tree in a dream, being tested in silence, not reacting to chasing figures — Buddha archetype. A test of inner stillness.
wakingMeaning: In a tension can you do 'just recognise, sit' instead of automatic reaction? This is the Buddha practice. Enlightenment is not a distant state — it is in these micro moments.
lesson: To awaken is not to flee, but to sit. To remain still is the greatest courage.

name: The Return of Quetzalcoatl
summary: Quetzalcoatl (feathered serpent) god of wisdom. He is exiled but it is said one day he will return. When Cortés arrived the Aztecs first thought he was Quetzalcoatl — a tragic confusion.
depthMeaning: Quetzalcoatl is the union of earth (snake) and sky (feather) — the marriage of opposites. The myth of 'one who will return' is the collective expectation of lost wisdom. But to recognise the return without knowing is catastrophe.
jungian: The Mayan face of the Self archetype. The feathered serpent is the perfect symbol of opposites' integration.
dreamMeaning: A winged serpent in a dream, a serpent transforming into a bird, expectation of a wise figure's coming — Quetzalcoatl archetype. A lost wisdom is being recalled.
wakingMeaning: Is there an answer, a guide, a return you have long awaited inside? Quetzalcoatl says: the one arriving may not be 'them'. You must distinguish false from real.
lesson: Know the face of the saviour you await. Otherwise misrecognition brings the real loss.

name: Pachamama — Earth Mother
summary: Pachamama is the Earth Mother of the Andean peoples — not 'else' than the globe, but it. Water drunk from her, corn eaten from her; all given to her, all taken from her.
depthMeaning: Pachamama does not personalise the earth — the earth is already a person. The modern environmental crisis is said to stem from forgetting Pachamama. Nature is not a 'resource' but a You.
jungian: The nature form of the Great Mother archetype. The limitless extension of motherly love: the world.
dreamMeaning: Putting hand on earth in a dream, sowing, harvesting, walking barefoot, speaking to a mountain — Pachamama call. The bond with nature wants to be reset.
wakingMeaning: Are you tired in city life? Would it heal to go into nature? Pachamama says: this longing isn't random. Walking barefoot on earth is a biological need.
lesson: See nature not as a resource you possess but as a being you belong to.

name: Anansi — Spider God
summary: Anansi is a spider god — owner of all stories. He bought them from the sky god with wit and trick. Trickster but wise.
depthMeaning: The Anansi myth is the power of storytelling. You may be a trickster but if you carry the story you are in the people's memory. In the horrors of slavery Anansi was carried to the Caribbean and Americas; culture survived.
jungian: The African face of the Trickster archetype. At the same time the sanctity of the narrator.
dreamMeaning: A spider in a dream, weaving a web, telling/hearing a story, a wit contest — Anansi archetype. Your creative intelligence is awakening.
wakingMeaning: In some situation do you need 'wit + a bit of trick'? Not a direct attack but a story-changing solution? Call Anansi.
lesson: Force alone is not enough; the one who can tell the story wins. Storytelling is sacred.

name: Call to Mab — Irish Fairies
summary: In Celtic mythology the Sidhe (fairies) live in invisible hills. Respect them, keep your word, else they cross your path. Their queen Mab governs dreams.
depthMeaning: Celtic fairies are the personalisation of the non-material yet real. The folkloric ancestor of the modern concept of the unconscious. Ignoring them produces symptoms; honouring brings healing.
jungian: Little personalities in the unconscious (complexes) — exactly Jung's view. If ignored they attack.
dreamMeaning: Small human figures in dreams, a fairy hill, songs sung by unseen voices, being called before a queen — Mab myth. A committee speaks in your unconscious.
wakingMeaning: Disappearance of objects, unreasoned mood swings, repetitive coincidences — Celts call it the 'sidhe effect'. Modern reading: the repressed pieces distort reality.
lesson: The invisible is real. Approach the unconscious with respect.

name: Cernunnos — The Horned God
summary: Cernunnos is a horned god who sits in the forest surrounded by animals. Owner of wild nature, guardian of cycles.
depthMeaning: Cernunnos is the antithesis of the modern masculine shadow — not aggressive but wild yet balanced. The masculine rooted in nature, who has no need to prove himself.
jungian: Not mature warrior — mature wild. The Celtic version of the 'Iron John' archetype Robert Bly wrote about.
dreamMeaning: A horned figure in a dream, a forest god, a wild but not-attacking masculine, a deer-man — Cernunnos archetype. The wild masculine inside seeks healthy awakening.
wakingMeaning: Often repressed in men: the wild side can be balanced rather than uncontrolled. Time in nature, body movement, physical labour feed this energy.
lesson: If masculine power has not been urbanised it remains wild; civilised it has rooted in the earth.

name: Galatea — Pygmalion's Statue
summary: The sculptor Pygmalion carves the perfect woman statue, falls in love with her. Aphrodite brings her to life; she becomes Galatea, they marry.
depthMeaning: The Pygmalion myth interlaces creation and love. But there is a shadow side: loving the image you create and refusing to see the real person. The ancestor of the modern 'manic pixie dream girl' syndrome.
jungian: Anima projection. The man chases his inner female image in the outer woman; the real person becomes invisible.
dreamMeaning: Animating a statue in a dream, falling in love with a perfect figure, then realising she isn't real — Pygmalion myth. There is a gulf between image and reality in a relationship.
wakingMeaning: Is the one you love really them, or your version of them in your head? The Pygmalion question is hard but transformative. Accepting Galatea as real is the beginning of maturity.
lesson: Love the real person, not your statue. Don't let your creation imprison you.

name: Tristan and Isolde
summary: Tristan, the king's nephew, while going to fetch his uncle's bride Isolde, accidentally drinks the love-potion with her. Forbidden love, secret meetings, two deaths at the end.
depthMeaning: The Tristan-Isolde myth is the root of the Western 'romantic love' myth. It says love intensifies when forbidden; the happy ending damages love. The first example of modern 'toxic relationship' alarms.
jungian: External projection of inner unreached opposites. The obstacle feeds love — the forbidden ends interest.
dreamMeaning: Forbidden love in dreams, drinking a potion, escaping from the king's gaze, two unable to unite — Tristan-Isolde myth. A bond is fed by obstacle in the unconscious.
wakingMeaning: If a love feels strong 'because it is forbidden', be careful — do you love the forbidden, or the person? Tristan-Isolde says real love also knows how to live without obstacle.
lesson: The forbidden is not intensity but dependency. Real love must be able to exist without obstacles.

name: Lilith — The First Wife
summary: In some old texts Adam's first wife is Lilith; she refused to lie under Adam and fled paradise. She is demonised, becomes a night demon. A figure reclaimed by modern feminism.
depthMeaning: The Lilith myth is the archetype of rejected feminine autonomy. The story of the one who fled rather than be unequal wife. Her 'demonisation' shows the patriarchal narrative itself.
jungian: Repressed wild / equal side of the woman. The Hebrew face of the Witch archetype.
dreamMeaning: Night, owl, a fiery woman, escape from the house, refused authority — Lilith myth. A 'rejected equality' inside wants to speak.
wakingMeaning: Do you feel secondary in a relationship? Lilith says: standing up is not demonisation. When you demand equality you may be labelled 'difficult' — this is the old story.
lesson: Being rejected is sometimes the start of owning yourself. Lilith fled but did not vanish.

name: Excalibur — The Queen's Sword
summary: King Arthur becomes king by Excalibur, given by an arm rising from the water. The sword tells us real authority is not external but authority coming from water (unconscious, divine feminine).
depthMeaning: Excalibur says real authority comes not from ego but from depth. The 'Lady of the Lake' is an anima figure — the inner confirmation that makes the king a king.
jungian: The king (Self) uniting with anima. Consciousness strengthens; words become swords but must return to water.
dreamMeaning: A sword rising from water in dreams, taking a weapon from a woman, receiving a gift and giving it back — Excalibur myth. You have a transition about inner authority.
wakingMeaning: Has authority been given to you in your life (job, parenthood, leadership role)? Excalibur says: this is not from your ego but from a deep source. For service, not ownership.
lesson: Power is not owned, it is carried. Know also when the time comes to give it back.

name: Earth Eater — Tiamat and Marduk
summary: The old goddess Tiamat (primal waters, chaos) is torn apart by the young god Marduk; the world is made from her body. Creation is born from destruction.
depthMeaning: The shadow of this myth: patriarchal order kills the 'wild feminine' to defeat 'chaos'. The body of the old goddess still composes our world — what is repressed is the original.
jungian: The new ego (Marduk) killing the old mother (Great Mother). A necessary step for maturing but a step deserving mourning.
dreamMeaning: A monster rising from a sea in dreams, tearing apart an old woman, victory of a young warrior — Tiamat-Marduk myth. An old paradigm collapses.
wakingMeaning: Are you consciously dismantling an old structure (with your mother, cultural pattern, an old belief)? You are in the Marduk state. But be careful: don't forget Tiamat entirely; she was the body.
lesson: Honour what you destroy. The new order is built on the blood of the old.

name: Persephone's Double Life
summary: Persephone is taken to the underworld, becomes Hades's wife. Half the year underground (queen), half above-ground (with her mother Demeter, the maiden). Seasons are this cycle.
depthMeaning: Persephone is the archetype of the adult who can live in two worlds. Both maiden and queen, both dead and alive. Wholeness is not 'choose one' but carrying both.
jungian: The state after individuation — consciousness and unconscious lived as two houses in balance.
dreamMeaning: Being half a year underground and half above-ground in a dream, double identity, going between two homes — Persephone archetype. You can belong to both outer and inner worlds.
wakingMeaning: Has your life split into two worlds? (Town-village, work-family, social-alone) Persephone says both are real, live both. Wholeness is not in one world.
lesson: Life is not one-directional. Be queen in both worlds.

name: Asherah — The Forgotten Goddess
summary: Asherah was the mother goddess of the ancient Israelite region, symbolised as a tree. She was later 'erased' in Hebrew religious reforms; her trace remained in sacred tree symbolism.
depthMeaning: The Asherah myth is the symbol of the divine feminine erased from history. When a culture's teacher is forgotten her trace lives in another form. Modern feminist theology tries to remember her.
jungian: Part of the collective shadow — recalled to the collective consciousness as 'the repressed returned'.
dreamMeaning: A great tree in a dream, an old temple, a nameless female figure, knowledge being remembered — Asherah archetype. A forgotten wisdom wants back.
wakingMeaning: Has the desire arisen to learn / live a culturally erased piece (your grandmother's tradition, an ancient knowledge)? Asherah is calling.
lesson: What is erased is not lost. Remembering can be a political act.

name: Hanuman's Service
summary: The monkey god Hanuman is so devoted to Rama that he carries mountains, crosses seas. His power comes from devotion; his ego is gone.
depthMeaning: Hanuman is the archetype of service: devoting yourself to something greater than yourself gives extraordinary power. The opposite pole of modern 'self-focused' culture.
jungian: The ego's surrender to the Self. Hanuman is not ego, but vehicle. That is why his power is limitless.
dreamMeaning: A monkey in a dream, carrying a mountain, crossing waters, serving a hero — Hanuman archetype. The question 'to what big thing do I want to serve?' awakens inside.
wakingMeaning: Are you able to do what you cannot alone when devoted to a cause? Hanuman says strength comes not from ego but from devotion.
lesson: Serve something greater than you; you will find strength there. When the ego melts it becomes wings.

name: Iktomi — The Proud Spider
summary: Iktomi is the spider god in Lakota mythology; clever but proud. He continually deceives himself. His stories are told to children as warnings.
depthMeaning: The Iktomi myth shows how intelligence goes wrong when it operates without ego balance. This story precedes the modern Dunning-Kruger effect.
jungian: The shadow side of the Trickster. Ego out of control combined with intellect.
dreamMeaning: A spider that weaves a web but falls into it, someone harmed by their own plan in a dream — Iktomi archetype. You may be about to fall into the well of your own intelligence.
wakingMeaning: Very clever but always falling into the same trap somewhere? Iktomi says intellect alone is not enough; it must be combined with humility.
lesson: Being clever is not enough. The one who can forget that they are clever is truly clever.

name: White Buffalo Calf Woman
summary: In the Lakota tradition the White Buffalo Calf Woman comes to teach her people the sacred pipe and rites. After bringing them she disappears by turning into a buffalo in four colours (black, red, yellow, white).
depthMeaning: This myth tells how ancient teaching figures arrive — an unexpected woman, sacred gifts, departure. A gift carried, not owned.
jungian: The higher form of the anima — a bringing teacher, then disappearing. Stays not materially but spiritually.
dreamMeaning: A white woman / an animal in a dream, a figure giving sacred objects but vanishing, four directions — White Buffalo archetype. You are being given a gift — don't possess it, just carry it.
wakingMeaning: Has a teacher figure arrived in your life and then left? (Person or experience.) The attempt to 'hold' her fails. Hold the gift, release the person.
lesson: You can be the carrier of a gift, not the owner. The going is natural.

name: Maui Catching the Sun
summary: The demigod Maui catches the sun with a lasso and slows it down. The day lengthens; people can do more work.
depthMeaning: The Maui myth says humans can negotiate with time — but carefully. Slowing the sun means changing the rhythm of work, leisure and life. The ancestor of the modern 'work-life balance' struggle.
jungian: Trickster + Hero. Courage + Cunning + The desire to change life.
dreamMeaning: Catching the sun in a dream, slowing time, stopping a force of nature — Maui myth. You want to change your perception of time.
wakingMeaning: Is your life passing too fast? Maui says: you can wrestle with time. Conscious slowdown, meditation, intentional rest — modern sun-catching.
lesson: Time does not change but your relationship with it does. Courage is also in slowing down.

name: Amaterasu Hiding in the Cave
summary: The sun goddess Amaterasu hides in a cave after the aggressive behaviour of her brother Susanoo; the world plunges into darkness. The other gods dance and show a mirror to call her out.
depthMeaning: This myth is the story of post-trauma withdrawal and return. The light (joy, creativity) hides; you call it back not by force but by play, music, mirror (reminding her of herself).
jungian: Temporary withdrawal of the Self (depression). Return requires love and festivity, not pressure.
dreamMeaning: A woman hidden in a cave in a dream, the world dark, a mirror with light, music and dance — Amaterasu myth. The light inside you wants to be called back.
wakingMeaning: Are you in a period of closing inward? Amaterasu says: don't force. Small beauties, music, smiling faces — these are signs that invite out.
lesson: The light inside sometimes hides; you call it not by chasing but by glittering.

name: Susanoo's Defeat of the Serpent
summary: The storm god Susanoo gets the eight-headed serpent Orochi drunk with alcohol and slays him with a sword. From the serpent's tail emerges the legendary Kusanagi sword.
depthMeaning: This myth says facing the shadow is the work of mind and resolve. Eight heads = eight angers / eight addictions. 'Don't attack directly, intoxicate' — the path to know the shadow is intelligence.
jungian: The hero defeating the shadow. Reward an inner gift (Kusanagi sword) — his own authority.
dreamMeaning: A multi-headed monster in a dream, an enemy defeated through intoxication, a valuable object emerging from a tail — Susanoo-Orochi myth. You are about to confront a complex shadow.
wakingMeaning: If you have a many-headed problem (each head you cut another grows) you live the Orochi problem. Indirect approach not direct; not force but the truth in the tail.
lesson: Don't hit the complex problem head-on. First tire it, then take from its tail.

name: Cuchulainn's Furor
summary: The Irish hero Cuchulainn, when entering battle, falls into the divine madness called 'ríastrad' — his body transforms, his power increases tenfold. Then he mourns, because he has killed many.
depthMeaning: This myth recognises the wild anger within us but does not glorify it. Cuchulainn wins the battle but the price is exhaustion and grief. Furor is also a gift but destructive.
jungian: The explosion of repressed masculine energy. Unrecognised it is suicide or murder; recognised it is the protector of the city.
dreamMeaning: Transformation of the body in a dream, uncontrolled anger explosion, extraordinary strength during battle — Cuchulainn myth. A repressed energy inside wants to speak.
wakingMeaning: Is there an anger inside that wants to explode? Cuchulainn says: that anger is real, but it needs a channel. Sport, expression, boundary-setting. Otherwise it explodes randomly.
lesson: Wild anger is divine but the price is heavy. Don't leave it without a channel.

name: Birth of Pegasus
summary: While Perseus cuts Medusa, Pegasus the winged horse is born from her blood. With his hoof he opens the Hippocrene spring — source of poetry and inspiration.
depthMeaning: Pegasus is the symbol of inspiration born from the 'shadow' (Medusa). The creativity that leaps when you confront the dark. Every artist has cut a Medusa and flown a Pegasus.
jungian: Creative energy born as a result of shadow work. Passing through the unconscious's frightening face, able to fly to the upper realm.
dreamMeaning: A flying horse in a dream, a winged animal, a singing spring — Pegasus myth. Inspiration is being born after shadow work.
wakingMeaning: A crisis passed — do you now want creation? Pegasus says: this is natural. If you hadn't entered the darkness you wouldn't recognise the light.
lesson: Inspiration usually is born from the shadow. Don't flee the dark — it is wings.

name: Achilles's Heel
summary: His mother Thetis dips the baby Achilles in the water of immortality — but the heel she holds remains dry. Years later Paris's arrow lodges exactly in that heel.
depthMeaning: No one is truly invulnerable. Even the greatest strength is fragile at one point — and that point is most often where the mother's hand was. 'Achilles heel' became an expression.
jungian: However strong the persona it carries a crack. That crack is usually the early childhood wound — it emerges at the critical moment.
dreamMeaning: Feeling vulnerable at a point in a dream, a small wound, an old baby scene — Achilles archetype. You are noticing a fragility you thought you didn't have.
wakingMeaning: Did someone seemingly very strong show vulnerability? Or did you live it? Achilles says: this is human — don't be ashamed, own it.
lesson: Don't think you are invulnerable. Even the strongest armour is open at one point — and this is its humanity.

name: Daedalus and Icarus
summary: Inventor Daedalus makes wings of feathers and wax for his son Icarus. 'Don't fly too high, don't fly too low,' he says. Icarus excitedly flies near the sun — the wax melts, he falls into the sea.
depthMeaning: Creative power burns itself when it doesn't know its limits. Icarus's tragedy isn't 'bad child' — it is the youthful exuberance that doesn't measure. Hubris (pride) is classical Greece's fundamental warning.
jungian: Inflation of the ego. Confusing Self and ego. The tragic end of the 'I am god' moment.
dreamMeaning: Flying in a dream but falling, getting too close to the sun, wings melting — Icarus myth. You are testing your limits in some area.
wakingMeaning: Did you rise too fast? Daedalus's voice: 'Neither too high nor too low.' The middle way is also winging.
lesson: Enthusiasm is beautiful but unmeasured enthusiasm burns. Fathers tell the 'middle way' — young don't listen, if they did, tragedies would lessen.

name: Medusa's Gaze
summary: Medusa used to be a beautiful priestess. After what happened in Athena's temple with Poseidon (rape), the goddess curses her — her hair becomes snakes, her gaze petrifies.
depthMeaning: The Medusa myth is the ancient version of 'cursing the rape victim' — extremely important for feminist reading. Cursed feminine power turns into one that 'petrifies' with her gaze.
jungian: The repressed feminine power becoming frightening. The 'shadow' projected by the wounded feminine.
dreamMeaning: A snake-haired woman in a dream, turning to stone, fleeing a gaze — Medusa myth. The wounded feminine inside wants to be recognised.
wakingMeaning: Are you judging a woman's (perhaps your own) 'hardness' as 'cursed'? Medusa says: look at the story — it is the protection of the wound.
lesson: Behind the hard woman there is usually a wounded girl. Ask the story before you judge.

name: Cassandra's Curse
summary: Apollo gives Cassandra the gift of prophecy, in return expects love. Cassandra refuses. Apollo curses her: her prophecies will be true but no one will believe them.
depthMeaning: Cassandra's tragedy is the symbol of intuition possessed but unlistened. The 'Cassandra complex' is known in modern psychology — especially the ignored warnings of sensitive / intuitive people.
jungian: The inner voice (anima) cannot reach consciousness. The disconnect between intuitive knowing and rational acceptance.
dreamMeaning: Shouting a truth in a dream but no one hearing, unable to prevent a known disaster — Cassandra myth. An intuition inside is being ignored.
wakingMeaning: Were you right about something but no one listened? Cassandra says: this is painful but don't stop trusting your intuition.
lesson: Speaking the truth is not enough — ask who will hear. But don't silence your voice.

name: Hercules's 12 Labours
summary: Hercules, driven mad by Hera, kills his family. For penance he is given 12 impossible tasks: the Nemean lion, the Hydra, the garden of the Hesperides, Cerberus...
depthMeaning: The Hercules myth is the symbol of the atonement journey after a mistake. Each task represents an inner piece — the physical monsters are actually inner complexes.
jungian: The classical map of the individuation journey. Each 'task' a shadow work, each 'monster' a complex.
dreamMeaning: Completing a difficult task in a dream, trial after trial, the number 12 — Hercules myth. You are in the middle of a long atonement journey.
wakingMeaning: Are you working through a series to heal yourself after a mistake? Hercules says: this is sacred. There is no shortcut.
lesson: Atonement is not punishment — it is transformation. The 12 labours are symbolic: the number of meaningful work.

name: Tantalus's Punishment
summary: For insulting the gods, Tantalus is buried up to his throat in water — but the water retreats when he bends; the fruit above flees when he reaches. Eternal thirst and hunger.
depthMeaning: Tantalus is the root of the word 'tantalise' — eternal lack of satisfaction. Everything appears reachable but always slightly out of reach. The metaphor of modern addiction.
jungian: Trying to fill the inner emptiness with external objects. The hunger that always says 'a bit more' but never sates.
dreamMeaning: Reaching but not grasping in dreams, unable to reach something very close — Tantalus myth. The feeling 'it is never enough' in some area.
wakingMeaning: After saying 'I have it' do you quickly want a new one? Tantalus says: satisfaction is not outside. Turn inward.
lesson: Eternal hunger's source is not outside but inside. Satisfaction is not an object but the tone of relation.

name: Atlas's Burden
summary: When the Titan Atlas lost the Olympus war his sentence is to hold up the sky on his shoulders. Forever — without putting it down, without resting.
depthMeaning: Atlas is the symbol of the person carrying the entire world on their back. The modern 'Atlas complex' — the one who takes everyone's responsibility, who never lets go.
jungian: The type-map of excessive responsibility. The illusion 'if I don't carry it, the world collapses'.
dreamMeaning: Carrying a heavy load in dreams, a mountain on shoulders, never being able to rest — Atlas myth. You have taken too much on.
wakingMeaning: Are you doing everyone's work? Atlas says: the load is real. But you must release it — the world will not collapse.
lesson: However real the load, carrying it constantly is inhuman. Learn to put it down.

name: Eros and Psyche
summary: Mortal Psyche marries the god Eros but is forbidden to see his face. Defeated by her curiosity she lights her lamp — Eros flies away. To win him back she goes through impossible tasks.
depthMeaning: The Psyche myth is the classical map of feminine individuation. To win love back after losing it she must gain her own wholeness. Love matures.
jungian: Bringing the anima to consciousness. Love going from 'given' to 'earned'.
dreamMeaning: Breaking a prohibition in a dream, losing your loved one, impossible tasks — Psyche myth. You are inside a love-test — it is worth it.
wakingMeaning: Are you on the path of losing then regaining a love? Psyche says: that love won't come back until you regain yourself.
lesson: Love is not given — it is earned. First earn your own wholeness, then love.

name: Argo Ship and the Golden Fleece
summary: Jason and 50 heroes sail on the Argo ship to Colchis for the Golden Fleece. Hera and Athena help. Every obstacle they face is another test.
depthMeaning: The Argonauts myth is the symbol of collective heroism. Setting out not alone but with a team. Each member has a different power.
jungian: Knowing our inner team — the team that is different archetypes (hero, sage, healer...).
dreamMeaning: A journey on a ship in a dream, a hard task together with a team — Argo myth. Not alone but with a group on a quest.
wakingMeaning: Are you working in a team? Argo says: each carries a different talent. Strength of not being alone.
lesson: The greatest journeys are made with a team. Solo heroism is myth; in reality it is companionship.

name: Oedipus's Fate
summary: It is prophesied Oedipus will kill his father and marry his mother. His parents abandon the baby; another family raises him. As an adult Oedipus tries to escape the prophecy and exactly fulfils it.
depthMeaning: The Oedipus myth is the tragedy of what we run from coming exactly toward us. 'What is repressed into the unconscious returns' (Jung). Source of Freud's famous complex.
jungian: Ignoring the unconscious drives us exactly where it wants. 'Knowing the shadow' is the way out of Oedipus's tragedy.
dreamMeaning: What you are fleeing meeting you, prophecy, being blinded — Oedipus myth. Something in the unconscious is being ignored.
wakingMeaning: Is there a theme you consciously flee (a type of relationship, career pattern)? Oedipus says: the more you flee the more you call.
lesson: What you flee is after you. Until you face it, it doesn't leave you.

name: Antigone's Resistance
summary: Antigone, despite the king's ban, buries her brother — conscience calls her. The king buries her alive in a tomb. She kills herself too.
depthMeaning: The Antigone myth is the classical telling of the conflict between state law and conscience law. Antigone is at the root of modern civil disobedience.
jungian: The stance of the super-Self (conscience) against ego and social order. Whatever the price.
dreamMeaning: Standing against a ban in a dream, at a graveside, being imprisoned — Antigone myth. Your conscience resists in some area.
wakingMeaning: Do you feel a rule is 'wrong' but obey it? Antigone says: there may be a price but conscience is not silenced.
lesson: Some laws are wrong. Conscience is superior — but be ready to pay the price.

name: Krishna's Flute
summary: When Krishna's flute plays, the village women (gopis) drop their work and run to the forest — to remember their divine love in a step. None remain indifferent.
depthMeaning: Krishna's flute is the symbol of the 'inner call'. In the middle of life's flow everything stops a moment — a sound, a scent, a moment calls you to 'the real'.
jungian: Hearing the call from the Self. The sudden 'spiritual moment' opening in the middle of daily life.
dreamMeaning: Distant flute sound in a dream, turning spontaneously, a divine attraction — Krishna myth. A call is rising from within.
wakingMeaning: Do you sometimes feel 'this is the right time' for no reason? Krishna says: this is the call. Don't refuse.
lesson: The divine call needs no reason. Pause for a moment, listen, change direction.

name: Hanuman's Leap
summary: Hanuman must use his power to leap across the ocean — but has forgotten. A wise one says 'you are Hanuman, you can leap'. At that moment he remembers and flies.
depthMeaning: The Hanuman myth is the story of the person who has forgotten their own power. Often another 'reminds' us — therapist, friend, teacher. With the call, power emerges.
jungian: The ego that has forgotten its own Self. Another consciousness playing the 'mirror' role.
dreamMeaning: Thinking yourself weak in a dream, then someone reminding you, then flying — Hanuman myth. A reminder is near.
wakingMeaning: Are you saying 'I can't' in some area? Hanuman says: remember — you can. A wise person will tell you this.
lesson: Forgetting our power is very easy. The ones who remind us are pure gold.

name: Shiva's Tandava
summary: Shiva as Nataraja dances in a ring of fire. The dwarf of ignorance under his right foot, his left foot in the air. Creation and destruction in the same dance.
depthMeaning: The Tandava dance is the symbol of the universe inhaling and exhaling. Creation is not separate from destruction — they are one dance. Even modern physics down to subatomic level sees this dance.
jungian: Integration of opposites — creation and destruction, static and dynamic. One of the deepest symbols of the Self.
dreamMeaning: In the middle of a dance circle in a dream, spinning around fire — Shiva myth. You are inside the creation-destruction dance.
wakingMeaning: Is an area of your life both being destroyed and created? Shiva says: this is the dance. Don't separate one from the other.
lesson: Creation cannot be without destruction. Seeing them as one dance frees.

name: Kumbha Mela and Amrita Manthan
summary: Gods and demons together churned the milky ocean — for the elixir of immortality, amrita. Four drops fell from a vessel; those four places are the sacred pilgrimage sites of Kumbha Mela.
depthMeaning: Gods and demons working together — duality transcended. The elixir comes from both good and bad. 4 points sanctified as the world's centre — basis of the pilgrimage tradition.
jungian: The opposition good-bad continues to churn in the unconscious; the 'elixir' (Self) is born from the union of both.
dreamMeaning: Churning an ocean in a dream, finding an elixir, seeing four points — Amrita myth. An 'elixir' (meaning) within is about to be born.
wakingMeaning: Does the 'good-bad' opposition exhaust you in life? Amrita says: let them work together — the elixir is born from there.
lesson: The elixir is not from pure good — from the churning of good and bad together.

name: Durga and Mahishasura
summary: The buffalo-demon Mahishasura, whom the male gods could not defeat, is overcome by the feminine goddess Durga, made of the power of all the gods. The battle lasts 9 days.
depthMeaning: Durga is the appearance of feminine wholeness when masculine power is insufficient. 'Unbeatable demons' are where the patriarchal approach gets stuck.
jungian: Higher anima — when integrated she does what masculine energy cannot. The protective face of the dark feminine.
dreamMeaning: A woman warrior in a dream, a ten-armed goddess, slaying a demon — Durga myth. The supreme feminine warrior inside is awakening.
wakingMeaning: Is the masculine approach insufficient somewhere? Durga says: try the feminine approach. It may be broader and more effective.
lesson: The masculine approach is not always enough. Mature feminine warriorship is a power to know.

name: Saraswati's Flow
summary: Saraswati is goddess of knowledge, music and art. She plays the veena (instrument), sits on a white swan. The flowing, unforced nature of knowledge and art.
depthMeaning: Saraswati is the symbol of knowledge flowing without effort. Not the obsession of knowledge but its holding. Science and art flowing from the same source.
jungian: The intuitive flow of creativity passing through consciousness. Not doing but being a channel.
dreamMeaning: An instrument in a dream, a white swan, a river, a book — Saraswati myth. The creative flow inside wants to be recognised.
wakingMeaning: Are you straining to create? Saraswati says: do not strain — be a channel. Knowledge / art is already flowing.
lesson: Creating is not forcing but surrendering. You don't push water — it finds its own way.

name: Ra's Night Journey
summary: The sun god Ra travels every day across the sky from east to west. At night he passes through the Duat (underworld) through dangers — especially the serpent Apophis. He is reborn in the morning.
depthMeaning: Ra's night journey is the daily cycle of consciousness death and rebirth. Every night a battle in the unconscious — a renewed self in the morning.
jungian: The conscious self descending to the unconscious at night and resurrecting. The archetypal map of sleep and dream work.
dreamMeaning: A journey in a dream, a battle with a serpent, dawning — Ra myth. You are living a night journey in the unconscious.
wakingMeaning: Do you wake tired? Ra says: night is a battle. Ritual is needed to resurrect in the morning.
lesson: Every night a little death, every morning a little birth. Carry it with ritual.

name: Weighing the Heart Against the Feather
summary: In Egypt after death the heart is weighed against Maat's (truth/balance) feather. If heavy the soul is feasted on by a monster. If light it passes to the immortal realm.
depthMeaning: This myth says moral judgement is not external but internal. The weight of your own heart gives the verdict. The ancient version of the word 'guilt'.
jungian: The super-Self (conscience) judging the ego. The guilt stone piling up in the unconscious.
dreamMeaning: Being weighed on scales in a dream, feeling the heart's heaviness — Egyptian myth. Your inner conscience is reaching a decision.
wakingMeaning: Is there a matter where you feel your heart is heavy? Egyptian mythology says: this is not conscience — it must be cleaned, the account closed.
lesson: Lightness of heart is virtue. A loaded heart is not free — cleaning is essential.

name: Set's Betrayal
summary: Set kills his brother Osiris for the throne, dismembers him. When his nephew Horus grows up he takes revenge. Set is left as the eternal god of chaos in desert and storm.
depthMeaning: The Set archetype is the Egyptian version of the 'jealous brother' myth (parallel with Cain-Abel). The symbol of betrayal in the bargaining of masculine power.
jungian: The tragic result of jealousy. Shadow reflection between brothers.
dreamMeaning: Your brother betraying you in a dream, a storm, being in a desert — Set myth. A bond contains jealousy dynamics.
wakingMeaning: Is there jealousy between you and a 'brother' (blood or figurative)? Set says: don't pretend it doesn't exist — talk.
lesson: Jealousy grows untalked. Recognise it early, own it.

name: Forging of Mjölnir
summary: The dwarf smiths Sindri and Brokkr forge Mjölnir, Thor's hammer. Loki, as a fly, tries to disturb them — the handle stays short but the power is complete.
depthMeaning: The greatest tools are not born from perfection but from being completed despite Loki-like chaos. Imperfection can be the condition of power.
jungian: The unconscious parasites (Loki) in the creation process — making despite them.
dreamMeaning: Disturbance while making something in a dream, a fly's obstruction, a short-handled hammer — Mjölnir myth. There are parasites while creating — don't give up.
wakingMeaning: Constantly meeting obstacles while creating? Mjölnir says: finish — even with flaws the power can be complete.
lesson: Waiting for perfection is the enemy of creation. Even if Loki's fly buzzes, cast the hammer.

name: Death of Baldur
summary: Baldur the god of light, loved by all. His mother Frigg took every thing's word 'not to harm him' — except tiny mistletoe. Loki finds this, lets the blind Hödr kill his brother with mistletoe arrows. After Ragnarök Baldur returns.
depthMeaning: The Baldur myth is the archetype of 'losing the most beloved'. A small oversight — a point not accounted for — can cause great loss. But the return is also real.
jungian: The idealised side of the Self (Baldur) — the trauma in the unconscious of losing him. The return after Ragnarök = completion of individuation.
dreamMeaning: Losing the most beloved in a dream, a great tragedy from a small mistake, then a return — Baldur myth.
wakingMeaning: Did a small oversight cause a large loss? Baldur says: mourn but you know — when the cycle completes the return is real.
lesson: Not noticing the tiny weakness of your ideal is a great loss. But every mourning is temporary.

name: Renewal After Ragnarök
summary: In Ragnarök the gods die, the world burns, sinks into the sea. But then a new green world rises — two humans (Lif and Lifthrasir), hidden in a tree, survived.
depthMeaning: Norse eschatology — destruction is not end but new beginning. 'Post-Ragnarök' is the metaphor of resurrection after deep crisis in modern psychology.
jungian: After the collapse of the old ego the birth of the new Self. The final stage of individuation.
dreamMeaning: End of the world in a dream, then greenery, two surviving sheltered — post-Ragnarök myth. You are renewing after a great crisis.
wakingMeaning: Have you felt at 'the end' of your life recently? Lif and Lifthrasir say: the seed hidden in a tree will build the new world.
lesson: No end is final. The hidden seed gives birth to the new world.

name: Dede Korkut's Word
summary: Dede Korkut is the wise bard of the Oghuz tribes. He tells his stories on the kopuz, gives children, matches couples, speaks words even at the moment of death. The oral source of Turkic wisdom.
depthMeaning: Korkut Ata is the Turkic version of the sage who 'builds the world by speech'. Not written but oral tradition — memory and melody carry him.
jungian: Turkic state of the Wise Old archetype. The voice of community memory and values.
dreamMeaning: A bard in a dream, an old playing a kopuz, storytelling — Korkut Ata myth. The wise voice inside wants to speak.
wakingMeaning: Is it time to tell a story of yours? Korkut says: speech heals. Let it not stay oral — say, write, share.
lesson: Speech builds the world. Telling a story is healing.

name: Manas Epic
summary: Manas is the legendary hero who united the Kyrgyz people. A 500,000-verse epic — the world's longest epic poem. Unity, the people, protection.
depthMeaning: Manas is the collective hero uniting a dispersed people. Not an individualised hero — the mirror in which the community recognises itself.
jungian: Representation of the collective Self. The answer to a people's 'who are we?' question.
dreamMeaning: Uniting a people in a dream, a flag on a mountaintop, gathering herds — Manas myth. There is a 'uniting' role inside you.
wakingMeaning: Are you in the role of bringing the scattered together? Manas says: this is a real call — own it.
lesson: Individual heroism is beautiful but being a community uniter is sacred.

name: Umay Ana — Turkic Earth Mother
summary: Umay Ana is one of the central figures of the old Turkic pantheon — fertility, child, birth, the earth. May be one version of the words 'hope' and 'ummah' in modern Turkish.
depthMeaning: Umay Ana is the Turkic earth mother — the local version of the same archetype as Pachamama, Demeter, Gaia. The symbol of abundance and protection.
jungian: The Turkic-Shamanic version of the Great Mother archetype. The wise feminine spirit of the earth.
dreamMeaning: Fertile soil in a dream, a mother figure, a warm embrace — Umay Ana myth. There is a need for maternal energy inside.
wakingMeaning: Do you long for maternal warmth? Umay says: you have the right to give this to yourself. Warm food, a sleep, a hug.
lesson: Maternal energy is not only in the mother — also in the earth, the embrace, a warm breath.

name: Yer-Sub (Turkic Water Spirit)
summary: In Turkic shamanism 'Yer-Sub' is the spirit of the sacred earth and water. Every mountain, every river, every lake has its own protective spirit. Respect for place is the fundamental rule.
depthMeaning: The belief that every piece of nature has a spirit is modern animism. Parallels with Pachamama and Shintoism Kami. The consciousness of 'belonging to this place'.
jungian: Nature consciousness. The thought that every place has its own 'genius loci' (place spirit).
dreamMeaning: Seeing a water / mountain like a god in a dream, talking with nature beings — Yer-Sub myth. A call to bond with nature.
wakingMeaning: Have you forgotten your earth-water bond in the city? Yer-Sub says: greet once — respect creates bond.
lesson: Nature is not lifeless — every place has its spirit. Respect creates bond.

name: North Star (Demir Kazık)
summary: In Turkic mythology the Pole Star is known as 'Demir Kazık' — the axis star of the sky. All stars revolve around it. Fixed, direction-giving.
depthMeaning: Fixed point — the centre of the changing world. The axis-of-the-universe myth exists in every culture but in Turkic 'iron stake' is an especially strong image.
jungian: The axis symbol of the Self. The inner fixed point — that around which all the changing turns.
dreamMeaning: Asking direction from a star in a dream, the north direction, turning around a fixed point — Iron Stake myth. A fixed centre is sought inside.
wakingMeaning: Is your life turning too fast? Iron Stake says: set a 'fixed point' — value, person, place. Let your axis exist.
lesson: Everything turns but the axis must remain fixed. Find your own 'iron stake'.

name: Birth of Isis and Horus
summary: Isis after her husband Osiris died gathered the pieces, magically conceived. To protect Horus from Set's threat she secretly bore him in the Nile delta, raised him.
depthMeaning: The Isis myth is single motherhood — the 'secret birth' of the dead husband. Christianity's Mary-Jesus myth rests on this theme. The sacred child protected under threat.
jungian: Solo motherhood, protective feminine, new potential born under threat.
dreamMeaning: Hiding a baby in a dream, hiding among waters, protective instinct — Isis myth. You are protecting a new potential.
wakingMeaning: Is something new being born (project, relationship, idea) but under threat? Isis says: protect. Not explaining, waiting is also a kind of protection.
lesson: Everything newly born is sensitive — if under threat, hiding to grow is legitimate.

name: Quetzalcoatl and Tezcatlipoca
summary: Quetzalcoatl (feathered serpent, light) and Tezcatlipoca (black mirror, dark) are eternal brother-rivals. Together they set creation, together they destroy. The basic duality of Aztec cosmology.
depthMeaning: Brothers cooperating beyond the good-bad distinction. Darkness and light do not destroy each other — each is the condition of the other.
jungian: The light-shadow balance inside us. In the absence of one, the other cannot exist.
dreamMeaning: Two brothers fighting in a dream, one light one dark — Aztec brothers myth. Your inner duality is working.
wakingMeaning: Do you see 'good-bad' two sides within yourself? Aztec mythology says: let them work together, don't try to destroy one.
lesson: Not to destroy your darkness but to integrate it with light — real wholeness is this.

name: Mayan — Hunahpu and Xbalanque
summary: Hunahpu and Xbalanque are hero twins. They challenge the underworld gods who killed their father — defeating them through death-rebirth games. The fundamental heroes of Aztec/Mayan cosmology.
depthMeaning: Twinness and joint struggle — not individual but dual heroism. Defeating the death-birth cycle is not alone but with a sibling.
jungian: Twin character; carrying the 'brotherhood' as complementary reflections of the Self.
dreamMeaning: Being in a test with a sibling, two figures dying and being reborn — Mayan Twins myth. A process of 'overcoming together'.
wakingMeaning: Are you in a hard process with a sibling (blood or figurative)? The Twins say: protect each other — you are stronger together.
lesson: Solo heroism is myth — real resurrections are achieved together.

name: Pele's Fire
summary: Pele is the volcano goddess of Hawaii. Both creative (lavas producing new land) and destructive (devouring everything). Respect to her presence is still given on the island.
depthMeaning: Pele is the pure form of 'destructive creative feminine'. Lavas destroy but new land is also them. The metaphor of volcanic love.
jungian: Wild anima/animus energy. Irresistible pull to passion but the cost is fire.
dreamMeaning: A volcano in a dream, flowing lava, a crimson female figure — Pele myth. The repressed wild creative energy inside is awakening.
wakingMeaning: Does a passion feel like it will burn you? Pele says: don't fear it. But know the cost — destruction too is part of creation.
lesson: The wildest creativity is also destructive. By recognising both you carry it.

name: Maui Hauling Up the Island
summary: The demigod Maui pulls an island (New Zealand) from the sea with his magical fishhook. The lands are his fish. The world created through heroism.
depthMeaning: Maui is both trickster and hero. The world not being as it is — someone must shape it. Creative hero.
jungian: The archetype of the creative hero. Refusing the status quo, changing the world by his own effort.
dreamMeaning: Fishing in a dream, pulling a land from the sea, a magical tool — Maui myth. There is a call to 'create' an area.
wakingMeaning: If you say 'no one does this' in some area, Maui says: then you do. Creative heroism is sometimes expected from you.
lesson: The world is not ready — someone makes it. The next could be you.

name: Anansi — Owner of Stories (extended)
summary: Anansi buys all the stories from the sky god Nyame — the price is catching the python, leopard and hornet. He succeeds with his wits. Stories are now in the world.
depthMeaning: A myth in which stories are valuable — and carrying them is heroism. Carried to the Caribbean / Americas in the slave trade: culture survived.
jungian: Guardian of collective memory. The sanctity of telling stories.
dreamMeaning: Buying a story in a dream, one weaving a spider web, gaining a knowledge — Anansi myth. In the storytelling role.
wakingMeaning: Is it your turn to tell a story? Anansi says: this is sacred — culture lives by story.
lesson: Stories are the life of culture. The teller keeps them alive.

name: Eshu — God of the Crossroads
summary: Eshu (Elegua, Legba) is the Yoruba god of crossroads, doors, thresholds. He carries messages. Usually the first god greeted — otherwise roads close.
depthMeaning: Eshu is the 'threshold guardian' — the African version of Hermes. Guardian of communication and transitions. Humorous but serious.
jungian: Union of Trickster and messenger archetypes. A bridge between consciousness and unconsciousness.
dreamMeaning: Standing at a crossroads or door in a dream, receiving a message — Eshu myth. You are at the threshold of a transition.
wakingMeaning: Are you preparing for a transition? Eshu says: greet the threshold first. Ritual opens the door.
lesson: Don't be hasty at transitions — greet the threshold, the road will open.

name: Yemoja — Mother of the Sea
summary: Yemoja is the Yoruba sea Orisha, the source of all motherhoods. The personified form of the Ogun river, when she was carried on slave ships to Brazil she became mother of the ocean.
depthMeaning: Yemoja is the limitlessness of motherhood — like water she changes shape, flows, never stops feeding. For millennia she has travelled from Africa to the world, still alive.
jungian: The African version of the Great Mother archetype — feminine consciousness surviving even in exile.
dreamMeaning: A woman on the ocean in a dream, white/blue clothes, being at the seabed — Yemoja myth. A need for the limitless form of mother energy.
wakingMeaning: Has your inner maternal side been exhausted? Yemoja says: it returns full like the ocean. But also rest is needed.
lesson: Motherhood appears limitless but returning to sources is essential. Water itself also renews.

name: Rainbow Serpent (Aboriginal)
summary: Rainbow Serpent is one of the fundamental creators of Dreamtime — source of earth, water, rain. She is respected; if angered, calamity comes. The artery of all life.
depthMeaning: One of the rare myths where the snake is seen as creative feminine. The spiritual state of rain and water cycle. The 'main artery' of nature.
jungian: Creative libidinal energy — flowing between earth-above and earth-below.
dreamMeaning: A coloured serpent in a dream, a rainbow, source of water — Rainbow Serpent myth. Creative energy is flowing — carry it with respect.
wakingMeaning: Do you feel connected to the life source? Rainbow Serpent says: carry with respect. Otherwise it withdraws.
lesson: Respect for creative energy is essential. If you exploit it, it withdraws; if you respect it, it flows.

name: Khidr-İlyas Meeting
summary: Khidr roams the land, İlyas the sea. They meet once a year on 6 May for Hıdrellez. Spring comes, a new cycle starts. A ritual still celebrated in Anatolia.
depthMeaning: Earth and sea, the brown and the blue, meeting once a year — the yearly renewal of masculine-feminine, passion-wisdom. The Sufi form of nature ritual.
jungian: The yearly union of opposites (hieros gamos). Inner wholeness renewed seasonally.
dreamMeaning: Two people meeting in a dream, a spring day, a meadow full of flowers — Hıdrellez myth. A moment of 'yearly renewal' inside.
wakingMeaning: Do you feel a year cycle? Hıdrellez says: do a ritual. An intention paper, a rose branch, a fire.
lesson: Honouring yearly cycles with ritual is a spiritual discipline.

name: Sol Invictus — The Unconquered Sun
summary: Sol Invictus 'the unconquered sun' was celebrated on the winter solstice (25 December) in Rome — the rebirth of the sun who dies in winter. Christianity inherited the date (Christmas).
depthMeaning: The solar resurrection myth exists in all cultures (Mithras, Sol, Jesus, Horus). The return of light at the depths of winter — the psychological yearly cycle.
jungian: The cycle of Self dying and being reborn. Archetypal celebration of resurrection after crisis.
dreamMeaning: Sunrise after a dark night in a dream, the first light after winter — Sol Invictus myth. A dark period is ending.
wakingMeaning: Is a dark period ending? Sol Invictus says: the light returns — celebrate.
lesson: The darkest moment is the herald of resurrection. Don't forget to celebrate.

name: Ouroboros's Breath
summary: Ouroboros — the snake/dragon biting its own tail. From Egypt to Greece, from alchemy to Jung, a symbol with a long history. Endless cycle, unity of creation-destruction.
depthMeaning: Ouroboros is the visual form of 'everything comes from everything, everything becomes everything'. Modern physics's 'law of energy conservation' is its ancient parallel.
jungian: One of the deepest symbols of the Self — wholeness and continuous transformation.
dreamMeaning: A snake eating itself in a dream, an animal in the shape of a circle — Ouroboros myth. A cycle is completing.
wakingMeaning: Is a cycle closing in your life? Ouroboros says: ending is a new beginning — don't think them separately.
lesson: Ending and beginning are the same moment. The circle makes room for consciousness.

name: Iduna's Golden Apples
summary: Iduna owns the golden apples that preserve the gods' youth. When Loki kidnaps her the gods begin to age. When she is returned balance is restored.
depthMeaning: For ideal sustainability continuous renewal is essential. Iduna is the symbol of the 'inner source of youth' — if you don't tend her, you lose her.
jungian: Inner life energy (libidinal flow) — if not consciously preserved it disappears.
dreamMeaning: A golden apple in a dream, water of youth, a gift lost then retrieved — Iduna myth. You are calling your inner liveliness back.
wakingMeaning: Do you feel tired, old? Iduna says: youth is not outer but inner. Which 'golden apple' did you lose? Take it back.
lesson: Inner youth is a resource — if you don't tend it, it is lost. Protecting Iduna is daily work.

name: Persephone's Return
summary: Abducted Persephone, by Demeter's efforts, returns for half the year. When she returns to her mother spring arrives. The myth of the season cycle.
depthMeaning: Persephone is the dual mother carrying both above and below kingdoms. In modern psychology the 'Persephone model' — the woman who can live in two worlds.
jungian: Descent-ascent cycle. Regular passage between consciousness and unconsciousness.
dreamMeaning: A season change in a dream, emerging from underground, meeting light again — Persephone myth. A dark period is ending.
wakingMeaning: Are you coming out of a dark period? Persephone says: this is yearly. Don't fear — but remember, the underworld is also your kingdom.
lesson: Life is not only in light — the underworld too is your kingdom. Carry both at once.

name: Birth of Athena
summary: Zeus swallows his wife Metis while she is pregnant — the prophecy says 'his son will overthrow him'. Then Zeus's head splits open; armed adult Athena emerges.
depthMeaning: Athena is the 'mind-born' daughter — absence of mother, consciousness self-born. The ancient version of the modern 'smart woman' archetype.
jungian: The full-arming emergence of the animus archetype into consciousness. The woman without mother, identified with masculine mind.
dreamMeaning: A woman emerging from a head in a dream, an owl, a wise girl child — Athena birth. The 'smart feminine' inside is awakening.
wakingMeaning: Have you become a very 'head'-bonded woman? Athena says: your power is real — but remember also the mother (emotion) side.
lesson: Being smart is beautiful but emotional root is essential. When together, Athena's power.

name: The Fall of Hephaestus
summary: Hera throws Hephaestus from Olympus for being ugly. He stays lame but becomes the world's greatest craftsman. Ugliness and skill in the same person.
depthMeaning: Archetype of the wounded craftsman — the unaccepted, known not by form but by creation. Many great creators come from a 'rejected child' past.
jungian: Masculine version of the wounded healer. The wound is actually the door of the talent.
dreamMeaning: Falling from a high place in a dream, staying lame, working in a workshop — Hephaestus myth. Despite your wound you create.
wakingMeaning: Were you rejected (family, society, academia)? Hephaestus says: that wound is your workshop. Not the rejecter but the creator stays.
lesson: Rejection is not a tragedy — it can be a workshop's door. Beneath the wound is craft.

name: Penelope's Waiting
summary: While Odysseus is away from home for 20 years, his wife Penelope weaves a cloth continuously and unravels it nightly to hold off the suitors. The endless waiting of pure loyalty.
depthMeaning: Penelope is the symbol of 'active waiting' — not merely waiting, the intelligence that constructs waiting. The pure form of loyalty and inner resistance.
jungian: Patient animus — conscious waiting, sustaining an inner decision.
dreamMeaning: Weaving-unweaving a cloth in a dream, the arrival of the awaited, a faithful watch — Penelope myth. An 'active wait' is inside.
wakingMeaning: Are you waiting for a decision, result, person? Penelope says: wait, but weave the cloth. Not passive but active waiting.
lesson: Waiting is not weakness — if active, it is intelligence, patience and hope.

name: Birth of Aphrodite
